title: JIT exception handling and stack unwinding
purpose: Compiled code must support try/catch exception handling. When an exception occurs in compiled code, the stack must be unwound to the nearest catch handler, with correct exception class, reason, and stacktrace.
task: Implement exception handling in JIT-compiled code using Cranelift's trap and landing pad mechanisms.

requirements.title: Try/catch in compiled code
requirements.spec: Translate try/try_end/try_case to Cranelift IR with exception handling.
acceptance: try block entry sets up a catch handler
Exception in try block unwinds to the handler
try_case receives class, reason, stacktrace in registers
Test: compiled try/catch catches an error and returns the reason

requirements.title: Stack unwinding from compiled to interpreted code
requirements.spec: Exceptions raised in interpreted code called from compiled code must unwind through the compiled frames.
acceptance: Mixed stack: compiled → interpreted → exception correctly unwinds
Compiled frame catch handlers are found during unwinding
Stacktrace includes both compiled and interpreted frames
Test: compiled function calls interpreted function that throws — caught in compiled catch

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement stack traces with source line numbers for compiled code — opaque frames only
SHALL NOT implement raise/reraise optimization — basic unwinding only
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
